body:
# Fastx.cr

[![test](https://github.com/bio-cr/fastx.cr/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/bio-cr/fastx.cr/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
[![Docs Latest](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-latest-blue.svg)](https://bio-cr.github.io/fastx.cr/)

Small FASTA/FASTQ I/O for Crystal.

## Installation

Add this to `shard.yml`:

```yaml
dependencies:
  fastx:
    github: bio-cr/fastx.cr
```

Then run:

```sh
shards install
```

## What It Does

- Read FASTA
- Read FASTQ
- Write FASTA
- Write FASTQ
- Auto-handle gzip when the path ends with `.gz`
- Stream large files with low-allocation readers

## Read FASTA

```crystal
require "fastx"

Fastx::Fasta::Reader.open("reads.fa.gz") do |reader|
  reader.each do |header, sequence|
    puts "#{header}\t#{sequence.bytesize}"
  end
end
```

`#each` yields owned `String` values. Use `#each_bytes` for lower-allocation
iteration with borrowed `Bytes`:

```crystal
Fastx::Fasta::Reader.open("reads.fa") do |reader|
  reader.each_bytes do |header, sequence|
    # header and sequence are borrowed Bytes (Slice(UInt8)),
    # valid only until the next iteration.
    puts "#{String.new(header)}\t#{sequence.size}"
  end
end
```

For large genomes you can stream each record line by line with
`#each_record_lines`, which never accumulates the full sequence in memory:

```crystal
Fastx::Fasta::Reader.open("genome.fa") do |reader|
  reader.each_record_lines do |name, lines|
    length = 0
    lines.each do |line| # line is borrowed Bytes, valid until the next line
      length += line.size
    end
    puts "#{name}\t#{length}"
  end
end
```

## Read FASTQ

```crystal
Fastx::Fastq::Reader.open("reads.fq.gz") do |reader|
  reader.each do |identifier, sequence, quality|
    puts "#{identifier}\t#{sequence.bytesize}\t#{quality.bytesize}"
  end
end
```

Use `#each_bytes` if you want borrowed `Bytes`:

```crystal
Fastx::Fastq::Reader.open("reads.fq") do |reader|
  reader.each_bytes do |identifier, sequence, quality|
    # All three are borrowed Bytes, valid only until the next iteration.
    puts "#{String.new(identifier)}\t#{sequence.size}\t#{quality.size}"
  end
end
```

`#each_record_lines` streams each record's sequence and quality line by line.
The quality field ends once its length matches the sequence, so the sequence
stream is consumed before quality (automatically, if you skip it):

```crystal
Fastx::Fastq::Reader.open("long_reads.fq") do |reader|
  reader.each_record_lines do |identifier, sequence, quality|
    seq_len = 0
    sequence.each { |line| seq_len += line.size }
    quality.each { |line| process(line) } # borrowed Bytes, copy to retain
    puts "#{identifier}\t#{seq_len}"
  end
end
```

## Write FASTA

```crystal
Fastx::Fasta::Writer.open("out.fa", line_width: 80) do |writer|
  writer.write("seq1", "ACGTACGT")
  writer.write("seq2", "TTTTCCCC")
end
```

Set `line_width: nil` to write one sequence line per record.

## Write FASTQ

```crystal
Fastx::Fastq::Writer.open("out.fq.gz") do |writer|
  writer.write("seq1", "ACGT", "!!!!")
  writer.write("seq2", "TTTT", "####")
end
```

`Fastx::Fastq::Writer` raises `ArgumentError` if sequence and quality lengths differ.

## Use Existing IO

Path-based APIs auto-detect gzip from the filename. IO-based APIs do not.

```crystal
io = IO::Memory.new("@seq1\nACGT\n+\n!!!!\n")
reader = Fastx::Fastq::Reader.new(io)

reader.each do |identifier, sequence, quality|
  puts "#{identifier}\t#{sequence}\t#{quality}"
end
```

```crystal
io = IO::Memory.new
writer = Fastx::Fasta::Writer.new(io, line_width: 4)
writer.write("seq1", "ACGTGG")
puts io.to_s
```

## Format Detection

`Fastx.open` is a convenience API based on file extension:

```crystal
Fastx.open("reads.fa") do |reader|
  reader.as(Fastx::Fasta::Reader).each do |header, sequence|
    puts "#{header}\t#{sequence.bytesize}"
  end
end
```

You can also pass the format explicitly:

```crystal
Fastx.open("output", "w", Fastx::Format::FASTQ) do |writer|
  writer.as(Fastx::Fastq::Writer).write("seq1", "ACGT", "!!!!")
end
```

## Behavior And Limits

- `Reader#each` yields owned `String` values you can store safely.
- `Reader#each_bytes` yields borrowed `Bytes` (`Slice(UInt8)`) and reuses internal buffers for performance.
- `Reader#each_record_lines` streams each record line by line as borrowed `Bytes` without accumulating the full sequence (FASTQ also exposes the quality lines).
- Values yielded by `#each_bytes` / `#each_record_lines` are only valid until the next iteration; copy them (`String.new(bytes)` / `bytes.dup`) to retain them.
- Readers are one-pass. Create a new reader to read again.
- Reader and writer instances are not thread-safe.
- `Fastx::Fastq::Reader#each` / `#each_bytes` support four-line FASTQ records only. `#each_record_lines` also handles multi-line (wrapped) FASTA and FASTQ.
- FASTQ reader and writer validate sequence/quality length equality.

## Base Encoding

```crystal
encoded = Fastx.encode_bases("AcGtNxyz")
decoded = Fastx.decode_bases(encoded)
```

Unknown bases are normalized to `N` by default. Use `strict: true` to raise:

```crystal
Fastx.normalize_base('X'.ord.to_u8, strict: true)
```

## Quality Encoding

```crystal
scores = Fastx.encode_phred("IIIIHGF")
quality = Fastx.decode_phred(scores)
```

## License

MIT License

constants.name: NORMALIZE_IUPAC_BASE
constants.value:
begin
  table = StaticArray(UInt8, 256).new(0_u8)
  table[82] = table[114] = 82_u8
  table[89] = table[121] = 89_u8
  table[83] = table[115] = 83_u8
  table[87] = table[119] = 87_u8
  table[75] = table[107] = 75_u8
  table[77] = table[109] = 77_u8
  table[66] = table[98] = 66_u8
  table[68] = table[100] = 68_u8
  table[72] = table[104] = 72_u8
  table[86] = table[118] = 86_u8
  table
end

constants.name: NORMALIZE_STANDARD_BASE
constants.value:
begin
  table = StaticArray(UInt8, 256).new(0_u8)
  table[65] = table[97] = 65_u8
  table[67] = table[99] = 67_u8
  table[71] = table[103] = 71_u8
  table[84] = table[116] = 84_u8
  table[78] = table[110] = 78_u8
  table
end
constants.doc:
Base normalization runs once per input byte. Lookup tables avoid repeated
branch checks and keep this hot path small.
constants.summary: Base normalization runs once per input byte.

class_methods.name: decode_phred
class_methods.doc: Converts a slice of Phred scores to a quality string.
class_methods.summary: Converts a slice of Phred scores to a quality string.

def.name: decode_phred
def.body:
String.new(scores.size) do |buffer|
  scores.each_with_index do |score, i| buffer[i] = (score + offset).to_u8 end
  {scores.size, scores.size}
end

class_methods.name: decode_phred
class_methods.doc: Converts an array of Phred scores to a quality string.
class_methods.summary: Converts an array of Phred scores to a quality string.

def.name: decode_phred
def.body:
String.new(scores.size) do |buffer|
  scores.each_with_index do |score, i| buffer[i] = (score + offset).to_u8 end
  {scores.size, scores.size}
end

class_methods.name: decode_phred
class_methods.doc: Converts an array of Phred scores to a quality string.
class_methods.summary: Converts an array of Phred scores to a quality string.

def.name: decode_phred
def.body:
String.build do |io|
  scores.each do |score|
    io << (score + offset).chr
  end
end

class_methods.name: encode_bases
class_methods.doc:
Converts a DNA sequence (`Bytes` or `String`) to a UInt8 slice,
where each base is encoded as a single byte.
When iupac is true, supports IUPAC nucleotide codes (R, Y, S, W, K, M, B, D, H, V).
When iupac is false, only standard bases (A, C, G, T, N) are preserved.
Non-recognized characters are converted to N (78u8).
This representation is suitable for byte-wise or array processing.
class_methods.summary: Converts a DNA sequence (Bytes or String) to a UInt8 slice, where each base is encoded as a single byte.
class_methods.args_string: (sequence : Bytes, *, iupac : Bool = false, strict : Bool = false) : Slice(UInt8)
class_methods.args_html: (sequence : Bytes, *, iupac : Bool = false, strict : Bool = false) : Slice(UInt8)

def.name: encode_bases
def.body:
sequence.map do |byte|
  normalize_base(byte, iupac: iupac, strict: strict)
end

class_methods.name: encode_bases
class_methods.doc:
Converts a DNA sequence (`Bytes` or `String`) to a UInt8 slice,
where each base is encoded as a single byte.
When iupac is true, supports IUPAC nucleotide codes (R, Y, S, W, K, M, B, D, H, V).
When iupac is false, only standard bases (A, C, G, T, N) are preserved.
Non-recognized characters are converted to N (78u8).
This representation is suitable for byte-wise or array processing.

class_methods.args_string: (sequence : String, *, iupac : Bool = false, strict : Bool = false) : Slice(UInt8)
class_methods.args_html: (sequence : String, *, iupac : Bool = false, strict : Bool = false) : Slice(UInt8)

class_methods.name: encode_phred
class_methods.doc: Converts a quality sequence (`Bytes`) to an array of Phred scores.
class_methods.summary: Converts a quality sequence (Bytes) to an array of Phred scores.

def.name: encode_phred
def.body:
scores = Array(UInt8).new(quality.size)
quality.each do |byte|
  scores << (byte - offset).to_u8
end
scores

class_methods.name: encode_phred
class_methods.doc: Converts a quality string to an array of Phred scores.
class_methods.summary: Converts a quality string to an array of Phred scores.

class_methods.name: normalize_base
class_methods.doc:
Normalizes a single base character to uppercase.
When iupac is true, supports IUPAC nucleotide codes (R, Y, S, W, K, M, B, D, H, V).
When iupac is false, only standard bases (A, C, G, T, N) are preserved.
Non-recognized characters are converted to N (78u8).
class_methods.summary: Normalizes a single base character to uppercase.
class_methods.args_string: (c : UInt8, *, iupac : Bool = false, strict : Bool = false) : UInt8
class_methods.args_html: (c : UInt8, *, iupac : Bool = false, strict : Bool = false) : UInt8

def.name: normalize_base
def.body:
if standard_base = normalize_standard_base(c)
  return standard_base
end


if iupac
  if iupac_base = normalize_iupac_base(c)
    return iupac_base
  end
end


replace_with_n(c, strict: strict)

class_methods.name: open
class_methods.doc:
Opens a FASTA/FASTQ file with automatic format detection or explicit format.
Yields the appropriate Reader/Writer to the block and automatically closes it.
class_methods.summary: Opens a FASTA/FASTQ file with automatic format detection or explicit format.
class_methods.args_string: (filename : Path | String, mode = "r", format : Format | Nil = nil, &)
class_methods.args_html: (filename : Path | String, mode = "r", format : Format | Nil = nil, &)

def.name: open
def.body:
if format == Format::FASTQ
  return Fastq.open(filename, mode) do |reader_or_writer| yield reader_or_writer end
end
if format == Format::FASTA
  return Fasta.open(filename, mode) do |reader_or_writer| yield reader_or_writer end
end

case filename.to_s
when /\.fastq$/, /\.fq$/, /\.fastq.gz$/, /\.fq.gz$/
  Fastq.open(filename, mode) do |reader_or_writer| yield reader_or_writer end
when /\.fasta$/, /\.fa$/, /\.fasta.gz$/, /\.fa.gz$/
  Fasta.open(filename, mode) do |reader_or_writer| yield reader_or_writer end
else
  raise(UnsupportedFormatError.new("Unknown format: #{filename}"))
end

class_methods.name: open
class_methods.doc:
Opens a FASTA/FASTQ file with automatic format detection or explicit format.
Returns the appropriate Reader/Writer instance (manual close required).
class_methods.summary: Opens a FASTA/FASTQ file with automatic format detection or explicit format.
class_methods.args_string: (filename : Path | String, mode = "r", format : Format | Nil = nil)
class_methods.args_html: (filename : Path | String, mode = "r", format : Format | Nil = nil)

def.name: open
def.body:
if format == Format::FASTQ
  return Fastq.open(filename, mode)
end
if format == Format::FASTA
  return Fasta.open(filename, mode)
end

case filename.to_s
when /\.fastq$/, /\.fq$/, /\.fastq.gz$/, /\.fq.gz$/
  Fastq.open(filename, mode)
when /\.fasta$/, /\.fa$/, /\.fasta.gz$/, /\.fa.gz$/
  Fasta.open(filename, mode)
else
  raise(UnsupportedFormatError.new("Unknown format: #{filename}"))
end

class_methods.name: open
class_methods.doc:
Opens a FASTA file for reading ("r") or writing ("w").
Yields the Reader/Writer to the block and automatically closes it.
class_methods.summary: Opens a FASTA file for reading ("r") or writing ("w").

def.name: open
def.body:
case mode
when "r"
  Reader.open(filename) do |reader| yield reader end
when "w"
  Writer.open(filename) do |writer| yield writer end
else
  raise(ArgumentError.new("Invalid mode: #{mode}"))
end

class_methods.name: open
class_methods.doc:
Opens a FASTA file for reading ("r") or writing ("w").
Returns the Reader/Writer instance (manual close required).
class_methods.summary: Opens a FASTA file for reading ("r") or writing ("w").

def.name: open
def.body:
case mode
when "r"
  Reader.new(filename)
when "w"
  Writer.new(filename)
else
  raise(ArgumentError.new("Invalid mode: #{mode}"))
end

class_methods.name: open
class_methods.doc: Opens a FASTA file, yields the reader to the block, and automatically closes it.
class_methods.summary: Opens a FASTA file, yields the reader to the block, and automatically closes it.

def.name: open
def.body:
begin
  reader = new(filename)
  yield reader
ensure
  reader.try(&.close)
end

class_methods.name: open
class_methods.doc: Opens a FASTA stream, yields the reader to the block, and automatically closes it.
class_methods.summary: Opens a FASTA stream, yields the reader to the block, and automatically closes it.

def.name: open
def.body:
begin
  reader = new(io)
  yield reader
ensure
  reader.try(&.close)
end

Creates a new FASTA reader for the specified file.

instance_methods.name: close
instance_methods.doc: Closes the file handle.
instance_methods.summary: Closes the file handle.

def.name: close
def.body:
if @io.closed?
else
  @io.close
end

file = @file
if file && !file.closed?
  file.close
end

instance_methods.name: closed?
instance_methods.doc: Returns true if the file handle is closed.
instance_methods.summary: Returns true if the file handle is closed.

instance_methods.name: each
instance_methods.doc:
Iterates over each FASTA record, yielding name and sequence as owned
`String` copies that remain valid after iteration.
instance_methods.summary: Iterates over each FASTA record, yielding name and sequence as owned String copies that remain valid after iteration.

def.name: each
def.body:
ensure_not_consumed!
each_record do |name, sequence|
  yield String.new(name), String.new(sequence)
end

instance_methods.name: each_bytes
instance_methods.doc:
Iterates over each FASTA record, yielding name and sequence as borrowed
`Bytes` (`Slice(UInt8)`).

The yielded slices point into internal buffers that are reused on every
iteration: they are only valid until the next record is read. To keep a
value beyond the current iteration, copy it (`String.new(bytes)` or `bytes.dup`)
or use `#each`.
instance_methods.summary: Iterates over each FASTA record, yielding name and sequence as borrowed Bytes (Slice(UInt8)).

def.name: each_bytes
def.body:
ensure_not_consumed!
each_record do |name, sequence|
  yield name, sequence
end

instance_methods.name: each_record_lines
instance_methods.doc:
Iterates over FASTA records while streaming sequence lines without
accumulating the full sequence in memory.

The yielded name is an owned `String`. `SequenceLines#each` yields
borrowed `Bytes` slices into an internal buffer; each slice is only valid
until the next line is read. Copy it (`String.new(bytes)` or `bytes.dup`)
to keep it.
instance_methods.summary: Iterates over FASTA records while streaming sequence lines without accumulating the full sequence in memory.

def.name: each_record_lines
def.body:
ensure_not_consumed!

lines = ByteLines.new(@io)
pending_name = nil

loop do
  name = pending_name
  pending_name = nil

  while !name
    line = lines.next_line
    if line
    else
      return
    end
    if fasta_header?(line)
      name = String.new(line[1, line.size - 1])
    end
  end

  sequence_lines = SequenceLines.new(lines, name, source_label, ->(next_name : String) do
    pending_name = next_name
  end)
  yield name, sequence_lines
  sequence_lines.drain
end

constructors.name: new
constructors.args_string: (lines : ByteLines, name : String, source_label : String, set_pending_name : Proc(String, Nil))
constructors.args_html: (lines : ByteLines, name : String, source_label : String, set_pending_name : Proc(String, Nil))

def.name: new
def.body:
_ = allocate
_.initialize(lines, name, source_label, set_pending_name)
if _.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(_)
end
_

types.name: QualityLines
types.doc:
Streams the quality lines of a single FASTQ record as borrowed `Bytes`.

The sequence stream is consumed first (automatically, if the caller skips
it) so the quality field's length is known. Each yielded slice is only
valid until the next line is read; copy it to keep it.
types.summary: Streams the quality lines of a single FASTQ record as borrowed Bytes.

instance_methods.name: each
instance_methods.doc:
Iterates over each FASTQ record, yielding identifier, sequence, and quality
as owned `String` copies that remain valid after iteration.
instance_methods.summary: Iterates over each FASTQ record, yielding identifier, sequence, and quality as owned String copies that remain valid after iteration.

def.name: each
def.body:
ensure_not_consumed!
each_record do |identifier, sequence, quality|
  yield String.new(identifier), String.new(sequence), String.new(quality)
end

instance_methods.name: each_bytes
instance_methods.doc:
Iterates over each FASTQ record, yielding identifier, sequence, and quality
as borrowed `Bytes` (`Slice(UInt8)`).

The yielded slices point into internal buffers that are reused on every
iteration: they are only valid until the next record is read. To keep a
value beyond the current iteration, copy it (`String.new(bytes)` or `bytes.dup`)
or use `#each`.
instance_methods.summary: Iterates over each FASTQ record, yielding identifier, sequence, and quality as borrowed Bytes (Slice(UInt8)).

def.name: each_bytes
def.body:
ensure_not_consumed!
each_record do |identifier, sequence, quality|
  yield identifier, sequence, quality
end

instance_methods.name: each_record_lines
instance_methods.doc:
Iterates over FASTQ records while streaming each record's sequence and
quality lines without accumulating the full fields in memory.

The yielded identifier is an owned `String`. `SequenceLines#each` and
`QualityLines#each` yield borrowed `Bytes` slices into an internal buffer;
each slice is only valid until the next line is read. Copy it
(`String.new(bytes)` or `bytes.dup`) to keep it.

The quality field ends once its accumulated length matches the sequence
length, so the sequence stream is consumed before quality (automatically,
if the caller skips it). Because the streams are not buffered, a
sequence/quality length mismatch is detected while reading rather than
up front.
instance_methods.summary: Iterates over FASTQ records while streaming each record's sequence and quality lines without accumulating the full fields in memory.

def.name: each_record_lines
def.body:
ensure_not_consumed!

lines = ByteLines.new(@io)
line_number = 0

loop do
  line = lines.next_line
  if line.nil?
    return
  end
  line_number = line_number + 1
  ensure_prefix!(line, 64_u8, line_number, "Identifier line must start with '@'")

  id = String.new(line[1, line.size - 1])
  cursor = RecordCursor.new(lines, id, source_label, line_number)
  yield id, SequenceLines.new(cursor), QualityLines.new(cursor)
  cursor.drain
  line_number = cursor.line_number
end

types.name: SequenceLines
types.doc:
Streams the sequence lines of a single FASTQ record as borrowed `Bytes`.

Each yielded slice points into a buffer reused on every line and is only
valid until the next line is read. Copy it (`String.new(bytes)` or
`bytes.dup`) to keep it.
types.summary: Streams the sequence lines of a single FASTQ record as borrowed Bytes.

class_methods.name: open
class_methods.doc: Opens a FASTQ file for writing, yields the writer to the block, and automatically closes it.
class_methods.summary: Opens a FASTQ file for writing, yields the writer to the block, and automatically closes it.

def.name: open
def.body:
begin
  writer = new(filename)
  yield writer
ensure
  writer.try(&.close)
end

class_methods.name: open
class_methods.doc: Opens a FASTQ stream for writing, yields the writer to the block, and automatically closes it.
class_methods.summary: Opens a FASTQ stream for writing, yields the writer to the block, and automatically closes it.

def.name: open
def.body:
begin
  writer = new(io)
  yield writer
ensure
  writer.try(&.close)
end

constructors.name: new
constructors.doc:
Creates a new FASTQ writer for the specified file.
Automatically detects gzip compression from .gz extension.
constructors.summary: Creates a new FASTQ writer for the specified file.

constructors.name: new
constructors.doc:
Creates a new FASTQ writer for an already opened IO stream.
IO-based writers do not perform gzip auto-detection.
constructors.summary: Creates a new FASTQ writer for an already opened IO stream.

instance_methods.name: close
instance_methods.doc: Closes the file handle.
instance_methods.summary: Closes the file handle.

def.name: close
def.body:
if @io.closed?
else
  @io.close
end

file = @file
if file && !file.closed?
  file.close
end

instance_methods.name: closed?
instance_methods.doc: Returns true if the file handle is closed.
instance_methods.summary: Returns true if the file handle is closed.

def.name: new
def.body:
_ = allocate
_.initialize(filename, idx, line, message)
if _.responds_to?(:finalize)
  ::GC.add_finalizer(_)
end
_
